description_en: Member disputes January late fee of KES 200. Claims M-Pesa payment was attempted on Jan 5th at 23:47 but receipt is timestamped Jan 6th 08:55 due to Safaricom network delay. Member has original SMS as evidence.
description_sw: Mwangi anadai alifanya malipo tarehe 5 Januari usiku lakini risiti ilionyesha tarehe 6 Januari asubuhi kwa sababu ya tatizo la mtandao wa Safaricom. Ana ujumbe wa SMS kama ushahidi.
evidence_refs: M-Pesa SMS (Jan 5th 23:47); Receipt RKT84932544 (Jan 6th 08:55); Secretary review of Safaricom logs
panel_members: Otieno Omondi (Secretary), Chebet Sang (Welfare Coordinator), Kipchoge Rono (UMOJA005)
resolution: Late fee waived. KES 200 credited to member's account. Safaricom network delay confirmed as the cause of timestamp discrepancy.
notes: RULING: Bylaw Article 5.2 specifies payment must be received by the 5th. However the panel applied a good-faith principle: where a member initiates payment before deadline and delay is attributable to a third-party system (Safaricom), the member should not be penalised. This interpretation to be codified as a policy note at the next bylaw review.

description_en: Member paid February contribution of KES 5000 to Treasurer Rotich's personal number instead of chama paybill. Treasurer did not notify the chama or transfer the funds until confronted. Member was charged a late fee for the apparently missing February payment.
description_sw: Awino alituma pesa kwa nambari ya kibinafsi ya Msimamizi badala ya paybill ya chama. Msimamizi hakupeleka pesa wala kutaarifu chama hadi alipoombwa. Awino alitozwa ada ya kuchelewa kwa malipo hayo.
evidence_refs: M-Pesa confirmation (Feb 5th, personal account of Treasurer); Treasurer verbal admission at Mar meeting
resolution: (1) Treasurer to transfer KES 5000 to chama paybill within 48 hrs. (2) Late fee of KES 200 waived. (3) Treasurer issued formal apology. (4) Chairperson to send circular reminding all members to use official paybill only.
notes: Transfer confirmed RKT86099001 on Mar 13th. Case closed. NOTE: This dispute also raised a governance question — the Treasurer at the time was Chairperson Kamau (dual role). New dedicated Treasurer Rotich Kiplangat elected at the same meeting. The dual role that created confusion is now corrected.

description_en: Member disputes April late fee of KES 500. Claims Safaricom paybill was unavailable on Apr 5th (maintenance window) when he attempted payment. Has screenshot of failed transaction (2:14 PM) and Safaricom complaint reference SF-CMP-2024-04-092847.
description_sw: Ombogo anadai huduma ya paybill haikupatikana tarehe 5 Aprili alipojaribu kulipa. Ana picha ya muamala ulioshindikana na nambari ya malalamiko ya Safaricom.
evidence_refs: Failed transaction screenshot (Apr 5th 14:14); Safaricom complaint ref SF-CMP-2024-04-092847; Safaricom maintenance notice MNT-2024-0405
panel_members: Otieno Omondi (Secretary), Chebet Sang (Welfare Coordinator), Kipchoge Rono (UMOJA005)
resolution: Late fee of KES 500 waived. Safaricom confirmed maintenance window 2:00–3:30 PM on Apr 5th. Member not at fault. Treasurer to reverse late fee in ledger. Bylaw note: force majeure applies where payment failure is due to payment system unavailability — to be formalised at next review.
notes: Panel cited consistency with DIS-2024-001 ruling. Confirmed that the principle of good-faith payment attempt applies when third-party systems are at fault. Ombogo welfare claim (WEL-2024-003) to be processed independently.

description_en: Member's welfare claim for child's hospitalisation was rejected by Welfare Coordinator. Member argues child was held in observation for 36 hours which should qualify under Article 8.2 (hospitalisation exceeding 3 days). Outpatient receipt was presented but member argues the 36-hour observation constitutes inpatient care.
description_sw: Kamande anakata rufaa baada ya madai ya ustawi kuhusu mtoto wake kukataliwa. Anasema mtoto aliwekwa chini ya uchunzaji kwa masaa 36 ambayo, kwa maoni yake, ni kulazwa hospitalini.
evidence_refs: Outpatient receipt (Nairobi West Hospital, 10 May 2024); Member's written statement of 36-hour observation
panel_members: To be determined at June SGM

description_en: UMOJA004 (Muthoni) and UMOJA008 (Chebet) verbally agreed in February to swap rotation positions (Muthoni: April, Chebet: August). Muthoni claims Chebet now denies the agreement. No written record exists. Muthoni received the April payout assuming the swap was in place but Chebet now claims she is owed the April slot.
description_sw: Muthoni na Chebet walikubaliana kwa mdomo kubadilishana nafasi katika mzunguko. Chebet sasa anakanusha makubaliano hayo. Hakuna kumbukumbu ya maandishi.
evidence_refs: WhatsApp chat screenshot (Muthoni to Chebet, Feb 12th: 'Uko sawa tukibadilishana? Aprili mimi, August wewe?'; Chebet reply: 'Sawa kabisa'); No formal Chairperson approval obtained
panel_members: Wanjiku Kamau (Chairperson), Otieno Omondi (Secretary), Rotich Kiplangat (Treasurer)
resolution: WhatsApp evidence shows clear agreement. Panel rules swap is valid. Article 6.2 requires written Chairperson approval — this was not obtained. Panel finds: (1) Swap is upheld as WhatsApp constitutes written communication under good faith; (2) Both members issued a formal reminder that future swaps MUST obtain prior written Chairperson approval per Article 6.2; (3) Chebet's position now confirmed as August. No financial adjustment needed.
notes: IMPORTANT PRECEDENT: WhatsApp messages accepted as written record for swap purposes. Chairperson will issue a circular clarifying the swap approval process and that WhatsApp evidence is sufficient but advance approval is still required.
